title: ageratina lobulifera
intro: Ageratina lobulifera, also known as Mexican tea or yerba de pasmo, is a medicinal plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. Ageratina lobulifera is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, or as a decoction by boiling its roots and leaves in water, allowing for its medicinal properties to be extracted and utilized.
